LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM WHITLEY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE. DEFENDANT WILLIAM WHITLEY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, BOTH OF WHICH WERE ENTERED ON JUNE 4, 2024, IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS TIMELY AND THERE IS GOOD CAUSE FOR THE RELIEF SOUGHT, ESPECIALLY IN LIGHT OF CALIFORNIA'S STRONG PUBLIC POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. MR. WHITLEY HAS UNTIL MARCH 28, 2025 TO FILE A RESPONSIVE PLEADING TO THE COMPLAINT. ORDER IS SIGNED. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William Whitley, 415-652-8122 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court informs Mr. Whitley of the court's ruling. The Court instructs the clerk to file the general denial of Mr. Whitley in Open court and to serve parties in the case. The clerk hands a copy of the genral denial to Mr. Whitley.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILLIAM WHITLEY's Motion To Set Aside.

Defendant William Whitley's motion to set aside the default and default judgment, both of which were entered on June 4, 2024, is granted. The motion is timely and there is good cause for the relief sought, especially in light of California's strong public policy of deciding cases on their merits. Mr. Whitley has until March 28, 2025 to file a responsive pleading to the complaint.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Continued to March 19, 2025. Opposing party is ordered to provide courtesy copies immediately in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. Failure to comply with this written order may result in sanctions or contempt proceedings. The Court notes that all parties are able to lodge courtesy copies.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
